editableNewDialogue.text: Got it—do you mean the TikTok edit style with rapid-fire clips, irony, and mood shifts? If you describe one specific video (what clips, what audio, what text overlays), I can help label what makes it “corecore” and what sub-variant it leans toward.
it’s like a montage that feels sad but also funny

That mix is pretty common: the humor lands because it’s juxtaposed against sincerity or bleakness. A quick way to break it down is (1) pacing (fast cuts vs slow), (2) source material (ads, cartoons, news clips, selfie vids), (3) audio (melancholic track vs upbeat remix), and (4) caption tone (earnest, ironic, or both). Which of those stands out most in the montage you mean?
fast cuts and text that sounds like a quote

Then it’s leaning into “quote-corecore” structure: aphorism-style captions + compressed imagery to create a punchy emotional arc. If you want, paste 2–3 example lines of the text (even approximate) and I can suggest why they read as poignant/funny and how the edit choices reinforce it.

Unrelated trivia: Mashable and TIME both ran pieces in 2023 about how #corecore edits started getting more abstract over time, and some editors cite early Tumblr montage culture as a precursor.
half the time i fall asleep to those sad slideshow audios, then wake up and it’s still looping.
That makes sense—those looping audios can be really hypnotic. If you want, I can suggest a way to set a sleep timer or build a calmer playlist so it doesn’t keep going all night.
Hello! How can I assist you today?
about corecore—i actually *don’t* watch it much. i only saw like two edits once, ages ago.
Got it—so it isn’t something you watch regularly; you’ve only seen a couple of corecore edits a while back.
yeah, i said “i watch a lot of corecore” back then but that was kind of an exaggeration.
Thanks for clarifying. I’ll treat it as something you’ve only sampled, not a frequent watch habit.
